title: eucalyptus leucoxylon subsp. leucoxylon
intro: Eucalyptus leucoxylon subsp. leucoxylon, also known as Yellow Gum, is a small to medium-sized tree native to southern Australia, characterized by its smooth, yellowish bark and small, white flowers. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oil, which contains compounds such as cineole and limonene that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Eucalyptus leucoxylon subsp. leucoxylon include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and decongestant properties, as well as its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of Eucalyptus leucoxylon subsp. leucoxylon include tea, oil, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
